home.title: Classic games to think, test reflexes, and keep your brain active
home.description: Play Sudoku, Snake, Slide Puzzle, and Tetris in the browser. Read articles to stay up to date, learn about the games, and discover their history.

articlesIndex.title: Articles, rules, tutorials, and strategies
articlesIndex.description: Learn to solve Sudoku with method, study advanced techniques, and visit the origin of the games available in Puzzled.

habitControl.title: Change Your Life by Controlling Your Habits
habitControl.description: Understand how habits shape behavior, learn the cue-action-reward cycle, and see three books that help build healthier routines.
intro: Habits are powerful tools that, when used correctly, can transform our lives.
Our habits define our behavior and shape our identity. That is why learning to control habits is a practical path for personal growth.
By understanding how habits are formed and how they can be changed, we can build routines that lead to healthier and more productive lives while learning from mistakes and keeping a growth mindset.

To achieve major long-term change, it helps to start with extremely small and sustainable habits, so easy that you have no excuse not to do them. Simple and achievable goals create a positive feedback cycle that supports healthier habits.
habitControl.frameworkIntro: To put this into practice, we only need three things: a cue, an action, and a reward.
habitControl.triggerBody: This is the moment or stimulus that starts the action.
triggerItems: Feeling a certain emotion such as joy, sadness, anger, or fear.
An event such as falling, receiving praise, receiving criticism, or finishing a task.
A word that is spoken or heard.
habitControl.actionBody: This is what we want to do every time the cue is triggered.
habitControl.rewardBody: If we succeed with the action, we can reinforce the behavior with a positive reward.
closing: If you notice that you are not managing to perform the action, make it easier. If you want to do 100 push-ups, start with 1 wall push-up. If you want to read a 500-page book, start with 1 page or even 1 paragraph.
It may seem like very little, but the important part is to create the habit and develop it over time. Small wins reduce initial resistance and make consistency easier.
habitControl.booksIntro: Below is a summary of three books that can help replace harmful habits with healthier ones, build new routines, and keep them with less friction.

books.title: The Power of Habit
books.description: Charles Duhigg explores the psychology behind habits and the impact they have on personal and professional life.
bullets: Explains how habits work through cue, routine, and reward.
Shows how to create new habits by starting small and practicing regularly.
Helps identify and replace unwanted habits without ignoring the reward that keeps them alive.
Connects habits to health, happiness, work, business, and collective behavior.
books.imageAlt: Cover of the book The Power of Habit

books.title: Mini Habits
books.description: Stephen Guise argues that very small daily habits can lead to major and sustainable change over time.
bullets: Suggests habits so small that you have no excuse to skip them.
Reduces procrastination and resistance to change with goals that are easy to complete.
Encourages increasing difficulty gradually after consistency is established.
Offers strategies to handle setbacks, track progress, and use positive reinforcement.
books.imageAlt: Cover of the book Mini Habits

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.description: James Clear shows how small accumulated improvements can transform identity, routine, and results.
bullets: Treats habits as the foundation of identity and life change.
Emphasizes incremental, consistent, and sustainable change.
Explores cue, routine, reward, and ritual as part of habit formation.
Helps overcome procrastination, resistance, and environmental barriers that get in the way of progress.
books.imageAlt: Cover of the book Atomic Habits

about.title: About Puzzled
about.intro: Puzzled is a straight-to-the-point site for people who enjoy puzzles and classic games.
about.introDetail: We bring together accessible versions of classic games and articles that explain their history, rules, techniques, and strategies. The idea is to use games to stimulate logical reasoning, focus, and concentration: with a Sudoku elimination, a better Snake route, a cleaner Slide Puzzle solution, or a calmer Tetris decision.

games.title: What you can find here
games.body: Each game was chosen because it combines simple rules with depth, making brain development possible in a playful and effortless way.
items: Online Sudoku with candidates, marks, print mode, and pattern filters
Online Snake with scoring, special foods, boost, and local best score
Slide Puzzle with numbered tiles, timer, and move counter
Online Tetris with tetrominoes, hold, preview, local ranking, and lines to clear

purpose.title: Our proposal
purpose.body: Beyond making the games available, Puzzled content explains where they came from, how their rules work, and which strategies help you play with more intention.
items: Sudoku tutorials from the basics to difficult techniques
Tell the history of the classic games available on the site
Stimulate logical reasoning, concentration, pattern recognition, and planning

audience.title: Who it is for
items: People who want to keep their brain active
Players who want a quick browser match
Sudoku beginners who want to learn to play without relying on guesses
Classic game fans who enjoy context and curiosity
People who use puzzles as an active break, focus practice, or strategy study

vision.title: What comes next
vision.body: We will keep expanding our library of games and articles, improving the game experience and the brain-stimulating challenges around it.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Use this page for technical issues, article suggestions, corrections, privacy, partnerships, or questions about Puzzled.
contact.emailBody: Send your message to the address below. When reporting an issue, include the page link, game name, device, and a clear description.
contact.topicBug: Report an error in a match, control, layout, ad, or Sudoku tool.
contact.topicContent: Suggest a tutorial, request a correction, or point out an explanation that was unclear.
contact.topicPrivacy: Discuss privacy, ads, analytics, consent, or site-related information.
contact.topicBusiness: Talk about partnerships, publishing, content, press, or business opportunities.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: At this time, Puzzled game features use the browser's local storage to keep preferences, progress, and records on this device.
privacy.localBody: Local storage is space inside your browser. Puzzled uses it to remember simple choices and preserve matches. This data stays on the device and browser where you played; it does not create an account and does not become a public profile.
privacy.localItemLanguage: Language chosen in the site selector.
privacy.localItemSudoku: Saved Sudoku state when a match is in progress.
privacy.localItemSnake: Snake settings and local best score.
privacy.localItemSlide: Slide Puzzle settings, records, and recent result.
privacy.localItemTetris: Tetris settings, high score, local ranking, and preferences.
privacy.noAccountBody: Puzzled does not ask you to log in before playing. In the current state of the site, game progress is not synchronized across different phones, computers, or browsers.
privacy.controlBody: You can delete local data by clearing site data in your browser settings. If you do, language, settings, saved games, records, and local rankings may return to their initial state.
privacy.changesBody: If Puzzled starts using accounts, a database, online sync, or new storage methods, this page should be updated to explain what changed and why.

sudoku.title: Sudoku
sudoku.emptyState: Choose the mode and difficulty to start a new game.
sudoku.checkCellTitle: Checks whether the filled value is correct.
sudoku.autoCandidateTitle: Fills the possible candidates for each cell automatically
sudoku.shareUnavailable: Unable to generate the share link.
sudoku.controlsBoardNavigation: Navigate and select cells with the mouse or the arrow keys. Hold Shift/Ctrl or use multiselect to expand the selection.
sudoku.controlsNumberInput: Fill cells with the number buttons or the keyboard digits 1-9. Use Delete or Erase to clear the selected cells.
sudoku.controlsModeSwitch: Switch between answer and pencil marks with the space bar or the Answer/Candidates buttons.
sudoku.controlsHoverHighlight: Hovering the number buttons highlights cells and candidates that match that digit.
sudoku.controlsSearch: Use the search field to highlight patterns, candidates and regions with helpers such as contains, unique, hiddenPairs, xWing and swordfish.

status.loadFailed: Failed to load a new game.
status.unsolved: The selected puzzle could not be solved.

gameOver.title: Game Over
gameOver.description: You ran out of 3 lives in 3-Strikes mode. Review the board or start a new game.

success.title: Puzzle Complete
success.description: The board was completed successfully. You can review the solution or start a new game.

printModal.title: Print version
printModal.description: Print the puzzle in its initial state with the share link and the game QR code.
printModal.qrCaption:
Open on your phone to play online
or check the answers
printModal.unavailable: Unable to open the print window.

autoCandidatesModal.title: Auto Candidates
autoCandidatesModal.description: Apply automatic candidates only to the current selection or to the whole board?

filterHelp.title: Filter Guide
filterHelp.line1: Use `&&` to combine commands and `;` to create independent groups.
filterHelp.line2: `contains(2,3)` highlights cells that contain any of those candidates.
filterHelp.line3: `contains(candidate=2|3)` does the same with named arguments.
filterHelp.line5: `only(1,2)` highlights cells whose set is exactly that.
filterHelp.line6: `count(2)` or `count(size=2)` finds cells with exactly two candidates.
filterHelp.line7: `unique()` highlights hidden singles within the filtered universe.
filterHelp.line8: `hiddenPairs()` or `hp()` highlights hidden pairs; `hiddenPairs(pair=1|2)` filters a specific pair.
filterHelp.line9: `xWing()` or `xw()` highlights the cells that form an X-Wing; `xWing(candidate=7)` filters by candidate.
filterHelp.line10: `swordfish()` or `sf()` highlights the cells that form a Swordfish; `swordfish(candidate=3)` filters by candidate.

menu.subtitle:
🐍🔥 Challenge your reflexes and push past your limits!
Every move matters, every point is a win!
How far can you go without making a mistake?

pause.title: Game paused
pause.description: Resume whenever you are ready to continue the run.

help.desktop: Desktop: use the arrow keys to move the snake, P to pause or resume, and the space bar to trigger the speed boost.
help.mobile: Mobile: swipe across the game area to change direction and hold your touch to trigger the speed boost. The swipe respects the configured sensitivity.

share.copied: Share text copied to the clipboard (Ctrl + C).

menu.subtitle:
🧠🔥 Test your mind and push beyond your limits!
Arrange the tiles in ascending order — think fast and prove you can master the sliding puzzle!

help.line1: Click or tap a tile adjacent to the empty space to move it.
help.line2: Complete the numbers in ascending order, from 1 to the last tile, leaving the empty space in the final corner.

share.message: I solved the {size} Slide Puzzle in {time} with {moves} moves.
share.copied: Share text copied to the clipboard (Ctrl + C).

menu.subtitle:
Sometimes life feels like a game of Tetris: pieces fall fast, out of order, and not always the way we want.

But every fit is a chance to organize the chaos, turning pressure into strategy.

Not every piece will be perfect, and that is okay.

The secret is not waiting for the ideal piece, it is learning to play well with what you have.

Take a breath, focus, rotate the possibilities, and keep playing. 🎮✨
menu.goalBody: Clear lines in sequence, raise the level, and hold your pace as time speeds up.

help.desktop: Desktop: use the arrow keys to move and rotate, the space bar for hard drop, C for hold, and P to pause or resume.
help.mobile: Mobile: swipe left or right to move, swipe down to hard drop, swipe up to hold, tap to rotate, and use the pause button in the top-left corner.

settings.description: Adjust pacing, piece behavior, and the sound layer without scattering disconnected options across the screen.

game.title: Tetris Canvas
game.strategyHold: Use hold to save critical pieces.
game.strategyPreview: Use the preview to plan your moves.
game.strategyHardDrop: Set the pace with Hard drop.
game.strategyTetris: Clear 4 lines at once to maximize your score.

pause.title: Game paused
pause.description: Resume when you are ready to continue the run.

share.message: I scored {score} points in Tetris, cleared {lines} lines, and reached level {level}.
share.copied: Share text copied to the clipboard (Ctrl + C).
